action.reasoning: Observation: predict.py is truncated (syntax error at state_tensor line + missing AutoTokenizer import causing NameError in init and test_obs failure); test_preprocess has truncation leading to NoneType iterable error on all variants; all real evals stuck at 0.0 success due to persistent processor errors and fallback actions. PI0 loads successfully per stdout. Analysis: Root cause is training-oriented DataProcessorPipeline (steps list of 6: LanguageProcessorStep mutates dict losing 'observation' for ObservationProcessorStep; AddBatchDimension etc. need dummy action + specific nesting). Manual tokens + step-by-step proc application with deepcopy/restore + complementary_data + PIL images (224x224) + padded state/action=32 matches guidance and test diagnostics. Complete non-truncated RecordingEvaluator (super init + evaluate_single_episode per contract) + main() for eval_config.json + exact JSON output + action chunking + tuned ee delta bridge will validate end-to-end. Mock test will confirm real policy actions if preprocess succeeds. This is the intended Pi0 adaptation track (no from-scratch). Decision: Overwrite predict.py and test_preprocess.py with clean complete code (manual tokens to bypass language mutation, step-by-step with error recovery prints, fallback only on true error, FULL PREPROCESS SUCCEEDED print), run both tests for local validation before submitting eval on base ckpt for first non-zero success_rate measurement.
